mnemonics: N.O.D.E. - Neighborhoods Open, Defined by Epsilon
keyPoints: A neighborhood of a point x is an open ball of radius ε > 0 centered at x.
Neighborhoods are the fundamental building blocks for defining open sets, closed sets, limits, and continuity.
The concept of a neighborhood provides a way to formalize 'closeness' without relying on coordinates.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that ε (epsilon) must be strictly greater than zero.
Confusing neighborhoods (which are open sets) with closed balls or other sets that contain the point.
